name: Semaglutide
evidence: Tier A — FDA-approved (human)
notes: FDA-approved GLP-1 receptor agonist for type-2 diabetes and obesity; once-weekly subcutaneous (~1-week half-life). Compounded versions exist but are no longer covered by shortage enforcement discretion (2025).

name: Tirzepatide
evidence: Tier A — FDA-approved (human)
notes: GIP/GLP-1 dual agonist, FDA-approved for type-2 diabetes and obesity; once-weekly subcutaneous (~5-day half-life).

name: Retatrutide
evidence: Tier B — human trials, not approved
notes: INVESTIGATIONAL triple agonist (GLP-1/GIP/glucagon); not FDA-approved as of mid-2026. Phase 3 TRIUMPH-1 positive topline (May 2026). Phase 2 (NCT04881760) studied 1/4/8/12 mg weekly — those are trial doses, not recommendations.

name: Liraglutide
evidence: Tier A — FDA-approved (human)
notes: FDA-approved GLP-1 for type-2 diabetes and obesity. Dosed once DAILY, unlike the weekly agents.

name: Dulaglutide
evidence: Tier A — FDA-approved (human)
notes: FDA-approved once-weekly GLP-1 for type-2 diabetes. Supplied in fixed-dose pens.

name: Cagrilintide
evidence: Tier B — human trials, not approved
notes: INVESTIGATIONAL long-acting amylin analog, studied weekly and combined with semaglutide (CagriSema). Not FDA-approved.

name: Survodutide
evidence: Tier B — human trials, not approved
notes: INVESTIGATIONAL GLP-1/glucagon dual agonist in Phase 3 (obesity, MASH). Not FDA-approved.

name: Mazdutide
evidence: Tier B — human trials, not approved
notes: INVESTIGATIONAL GLP-1/glucagon dual agonist (GcgR/GLP-1R). Not FDA-approved.

name: Tesamorelin
evidence: Tier A — FDA-approved (human)
notes: The ONLY FDA-approved molecule among the research peptides (for HIV-associated lipodystrophy). Labeled once-daily subcutaneous: Egrifta 2 mg / Egrifta SV 1.4 mg / Egrifta WR 1.28 mg. WADA-prohibited.

name: CJC-1295 (DAC)
evidence: Tier B — human trials, not approved
notes: GHRH analog with a Drug Affinity Complex that greatly extends its half-life (long-acting, roughly weekly in community use). Distinct from the no-DAC version. WADA-prohibited.

name: CJC-1295 (no DAC)
evidence: Tier B — human trials, not approved
notes: Short-acting GHRH analog (~30 min half-life), used more frequently than the DAC version. Distinct from CJC-1295 (DAC). WADA-prohibited.

name: Ipamorelin
evidence: Tier B — human trials, not approved
notes: Selective ghrelin-receptor / GH secretagogue. Per FDA it is 503A Category 1 — a different status than the peptides removed from Category 2 in April 2026. WADA-prohibited.

name: Sermorelin
evidence: Tier B — human trials, not approved
notes: GHRH(1-29) analog; the branded product Geref was discontinued. Human PK data exist; not currently an approved product. WADA-prohibited.

name: GHRP-2
evidence: Tier B — human trials, not approved
notes: GH-releasing peptide (ghrelin mimetic). Used diagnostically as pralmorelin abroad; not FDA-approved for therapy. WADA-prohibited.

name: GHRP-6
evidence: Tier B — human trials, not approved
notes: GH-releasing peptide; strongly increases appetite via ghrelin signaling. Not FDA-approved. WADA-prohibited.

name: Hexarelin
evidence: Tier B — human trials, not approved
notes: Potent GH-releasing peptide; the GH response can desensitize with continued use. Not FDA-approved. WADA-prohibited.

name: MK-677
evidence: Tier B — human trials, not approved
notes: Orally active ghrelin-receptor agonist (NOT injected). Studied in humans but never approved; can raise appetite, blood glucose, and water retention. WADA-prohibited.

name: BPC-157
evidence: Tier C — preclinical / no completed human trials
notes: No completed Phase II trial; human data come from fewer than 30 subjects across uncontrolled studies. Removed from FDA 503A Category 2 (April 2026, procedural). WADA-prohibited.

name: TB-500
evidence: Tier C — preclinical / no completed human trials
notes: IMPORTANT CORRECTION: TB-500 is the synthetic Ac-LKKTETQ fragment, NOT full-length thymosin beta-4. Preclinical only. WADA-prohibited.

name: Thymosin Beta-4
evidence: Tier C — preclinical / no completed human trials
notes: Full-length 43-amino-acid peptide, distinct from the TB-500 fragment. Preclinical / early-trial only. WADA-prohibited.

name: Thymosin Alpha-1
evidence: Tier B — human trials, not approved
notes: Immune-modulating peptide; approved in some countries as Zadaxin but not FDA-approved. Human trials exist across several indications.

name: KPV
evidence: Tier C — preclinical / no completed human trials
notes: Tripeptide fragment of α-MSH studied preclinically for anti-inflammatory effects. No approved human product.

name: LL-37
evidence: Tier C — preclinical / no completed human trials
notes: Antimicrobial host-defense peptide studied preclinically. No approved human product; injectable use is unstudied.

name: GHK-Cu (injectable)
evidence: Tier D — precursor/topical evidence, injected off-label
notes: Human evidence is largely for TOPICAL GHK-Cu; injectable use is off-label and unstudied.

name: PT-141
evidence: Tier A — FDA-approved (human)
notes: Melanocortin agonist; FDA-approved as Vyleesi (1.75 mg subcutaneous) for premenopausal HSDD. Can transiently raise blood pressure and cause nausea/flushing.

name: Melanotan II
evidence: Tier C — preclinical / no completed human trials
notes: Non-selective melanocortin agonist used for tanning/libido; NOT approved. Linked to nausea and to darkening/changing moles — the literature advises dermatologic monitoring.

name: Epithalon
evidence: Tier C — preclinical / no completed human trials
notes: Synthetic tetrapeptide studied (mostly in Russian literature) for telomerase/longevity claims. No robust independent human evidence; not approved.

name: AOD-9604
evidence: Tier C — preclinical / no completed human trials
notes: GH fragment (176-191) marketed for fat loss; human obesity trials did NOT show meaningful weight loss versus placebo. Not approved.

name: MOTS-c
evidence: Tier C — preclinical / no completed human trials
notes: Mitochondrial-derived peptide studied preclinically for metabolism/exercise. No approved human product.

name: NAD+
evidence: Tier D — precursor/topical evidence, injected off-label
notes: CORRECTION: NAD+ is a dinucleotide, NOT a peptide. Injected doses are large (tens of mg) and often cause flushing/discomfort if pushed too fast.

name: Glutathione
evidence: Tier D — precursor/topical evidence, injected off-label
notes: Antioxidant tripeptide (not a signaling peptide). Injected off-label for skin/wellness; robust human efficacy evidence is limited.

name: DSIP
evidence: Tier C — preclinical / no completed human trials
notes: Nonapeptide studied for sleep/stress with inconsistent results. No approved human product.

name: Selank
evidence: Tier B — human trials, not approved
notes: Anxiolytic peptide developed in Russia (human use there); not FDA-approved. Often used intranasally.

name: Semax
evidence: Tier B — human trials, not approved
notes: Nootropic/neuroprotective peptide developed in Russia (human use there); not FDA-approved. Often used intranasally.

name: IGF-1 LR3
evidence: Tier C — preclinical / no completed human trials
notes: Modified IGF-1 with an extended half-life. WADA-prohibited (S2). No approved human product; growth-factor signaling carries theoretical cancer-risk concerns.
